requiredBehaviors: recognizes bash, sh, and zsh -c/-lc wrappers by executable basename
parses literal word-only command sequences joined by &&, ||, |, semicolon, and newlines
rejects redirection, env assignment, substitutions, malformed operators, unterminated quotes, and background operators
recovers a single command prefix before heredoc bodies without tokenizing heredoc data
edgeCases: opaque bash wrappers canonicalize with an AgenC marker instead of granting a safe argv
multi-command wrappers are parsed for summaries but remain opaque for single-command approval cache matching
heredoc prefix recovery rejects env assignments and multi-command prefixes

requiredBehaviors: summarizes read, list-files, and search command families
tracks cd prefixes inside word-only shell wrappers
collapses the whole command to unknown when any retained segment is unknown
edgeCases: small formatting helpers can be dropped from summaries
PowerShell wrappers remain opaque for summaries until native AST parsing is explicitly used by safety

requiredBehaviors: rejects git global options and output flags that alter lookup or write files
rejects find exec/delete/fprint, base64 output, and ripgrep pre/zip helper flags
recurses through word-only Bash wrappers and requires every child command to be safe
edgeCases: git branch is safe only for flag-only inspection forms
sed is safe only for -n print-range scripts
interpreter and package-manager commands from the older sandbox allowlist are no longer auto-approved

requiredBehaviors: recurses through sudo and word-only Bash wrappers
feeds the Bash permission hard-floor denial path
edgeCases: non-word-only shell scripts stay with the existing recursive shell-fragment detector
safe read-only commands do not become dangerous solely because they are parsed by the new layer

requiredBehaviors: parses literal PowerShell scripts with the real platform AST parser when available
rejects dynamic PowerShell constructs, redirection, invocation operators, and parser errors
detects Windows URL launchers and forced deletion commands
allows only literal read-only PowerShell commands and read-only git/rg forms
edgeCases: missing PowerShell executable returns a failed outcome instead of throwing or approving
URL launcher tests use the AgenC-owned domain
non-Windows hosts do not approximate PowerShell safe auto-approval

commands: npm test --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime -- shell-command/parser.test.ts shell-command/safety.test.ts shell-command/powershell-parser.test.ts permissions/bash.test.ts permissions/approval-cache.test.ts
